preamble: The transformation from a voiced velar fricative to an alveolar flap is a specific type of velar-to-rhotic rhotacism, often occurring via an intermediate uvular stage or through direct place-of-articulation drift.

examples.note: An unconditional sound law where the voiced velar fricative shifted to an alveolar rhotic.
While the inverse shift, dialects like Kedah and Terengganu show the fluid boundary between these sounds where the rhotic stabilizes as a velar fricative.

sources: Thelwall, R. (1981). The Daju Language Group.
Collins, J. T. (1986). Dialek Melayu Kedah.
